decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to quickly get out Omniscience onto the battlefield using Show and Tell or Cunning Wish, allowing you to cast spells for free. This deck utilizes powerful combo pieces such as Auroral Procession and Stock Up to generate a large amount of creatures or draw cards for free. Additionally, cards like Veil of Summer and Flusterstorm provide protection against opposing decks trying to disrupt your combo. Overall, the goal is to establish a dominant board presence and overwhelm your opponent with powerful spells and creatures.

This deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly using Gaea's Cradle and creatures like Birds of Paradise and Ignoble Hierarch. It utilizes Natural Order to search for powerful creatures like Craterhoof Behemoth and Grist, the Hunger Tide to close out games. The deck also includes disruption with Wasteland and Collector Ouphe to control the opponent's board. Additionally, it has resilience with cards like Endurance and Ba Sing Se to protect its own creatures. Overall, the strategy revolves around efficiently ramping up mana and deploying threats to overpower the opponent.

The deck focuses on using powerful ramp cards like Cloudpost and Tron lands to quickly generate mana to cast high-cost spells and overwhelming threats. It utilizes control elements such as counterspells and removal to disrupt opponent's game plan and protect its own win conditions. The deck aims to establish a dominant board presence with powerful Eldrazi creatures and planeswalkers like Ugin, the Spirit Dragon. It aims to control the game, disrupt opponent's strategy, and overwhelm them with powerful late-game threats to secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building card advantage and generating value with cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Lórien Revealed. It aims to control the game through counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce, while also utilizing removal spells such as Swords to Plowshares to deal with threats. The deck also has access to a variety of cantrips like Ponder and Brainstorm to dig for key cards and maintain consistency. Additionally, the deck has access to powerful finishers like Murktide Regent to close out the game once control has been established.

The deck focuses on utilizing disruptive creatures and spells to slow down opponents while also providing aggressive threats to finish off the game quickly. By efficiently disrupting the opponent's game plan and deploying powerful creatures, the deck aims to control the tempo of the game and overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. The use of cards like Stoneforge Mystic, Skyclave Apparition, and Recruiter of the Guard allows for strong board presence and flexibility in handling various situations. The deck's strategy is to disrupt, outvalue, and pressure the opponent to secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty, then use spells like Reanimate, Shallow Grave, and Animate Dead to bring them back into play for a quick and powerful attack. Cards like Unmask and Thoughtseize help to disrupt the opponent's strategy while Faithless Looting and Lotus Petal provide card draw and mana acceleration. The inclusion of planeswalkers like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and powerful creatures like Koma, World-Eater provide additional threats and utility to the deck.

The main focus of this deck is to lock down the opponent's resources and disrupt their gameplay using artifact-based control elements. By utilizing cards like Urza's Saga, Planar Nexus, Karn, the Great Creator, and Trinisphere, the deck aims to create a prison-like environment for the opponent, limiting their options and preventing them from executing their strategy effectively. Additionally, the inclusion of powerful artifacts such as The One Ring and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn provides a win condition for the deck, ensuring that it can close out the game once the opponent is sufficiently constrained.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through counterspells and removal spells while building card advantage and controlling the board. The deck's win conditions include Murktide Regent and various artifacts to close out the game. The key strategy is to maintain control of the game by efficiently answering threats and consistently drawing cards to stay ahead in resources. The deck aims to outlast the opponent and grind out a victory through careful decision-making and resource management.

Utilize the combo of Show and Tell with Sneak Attack to cheat big creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play quickly. Use counterspells like Force of Will to protect your combo, while also using cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder to find the pieces you need. Generate mana with Lotus Petal, Stock Up, and lands like Ancient Tomb and City of Traitors. The deck is designed to quickly assemble your combo pieces and overwhelm your opponent with powerful creatures before they can react.

The key concept of this Dimir Tempo deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining pressure on the board through efficient removal spells and disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will. The deck aims to control the tempo of the game by using cards like Daze and Flow State to keep the opponent on the back foot, while also deploying threats like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Nethergoyf to close out the game quickly. With a mix of counterspells, removal, and card draw, this deck seeks to establish dominance through a combination of aggression and control elements.

The key concept of this UWx Control deck is to maintain control over the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while building up its own resources with cards like Flow State, Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, and Jace, the Mind Sculptor. By efficiently managing threats with cards like Swords to Plowshares and Terminus, and leveraging powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Time Raveler, the deck looks to dictate the pace of the game and ultimately win through incremental advantage.

The deck focuses on using artifacts to create a prison-like control over the opponent, locking down their resources and preventing them from executing their game plan effectively. By utilizing powerful artifacts such as Ugin, Eye of the Storm, Trinisphere, and Karn, the Great Creator, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and ultimately overpower them with big threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Cityscape Leveler. The key strategy involves setting up a strong board presence with artifacts while denying the opponent the ability to counter or remove them easily.

The deck revolves around quickly getting powerful creatures onto the battlefield by using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. By cheating out big threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Griselbrand, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to respond. The deck also includes protection spells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to ensure that the combo can go off smoothly. The key is to efficiently get these threats onto the field and control the game through powerful creatures and game-changing effects.

The deck focuses on quickly putting powerful creatures into play from the graveyard, combining cards like Animate Dead, Reanimate, and Shallow Grave with powerful creatures like Griselbrand, Koma, and Atraxa. It also includes disruption in the form of Thoughtseize, Collective Brutality, and Unmask to protect the combo. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with big threats early in the game, taking advantage of the graveyard as a resource. With a mix of disruption and powerful reanimation spells, the deck is designed to quickly close out games before the opponent can react.

The deck focuses on locking down the opponent's options by using artifacts like Candelabra of Tawnos and Trinisphere, along with powerful planeswalkers like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, Eye of the Storms. It also includes powerful threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn to close out the game. By utilizing cards like Urza's Saga and Urza's Workshop, the deck can quickly assemble a powerful board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to control the game through the use of artifacts and powerful prison effects.

The main concept of this Mono Black Aggro deck is to quickly disrupt your opponent's hand with cards like Thoughtseize and Hymn to Tourach, while also efficiently removing threats with cards like Fatal Push and Snuff Out. The deck aims to apply pressure early with aggressive creatures like Dauthi Voidwalker and Nethergoyf, backed up by disruption and removal spells. The inclusion of cards like Urza's Bauble and Mishra's Bauble helps to smooth out draws and maintain card advantage. The deck also utilizes lands like Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base and gain a tempo advantage.
